ddls:
CREATE TABLE "operators" (
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operator */,
  "operator_name" TEXT /* Name of the operator (e.g., +, -, *, /) */,
  "order_of_precedence" INTEGER /* Order of precedence of the operator */,
  "operator_arity" TEXT /* Arity of the operator (Unary, Binary) */,
  "input_data_types" TEXT /* Data types that the operator can process */,
  "operator_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of the operator */,
  "precedence_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the precedence level of the operator */,
  "is_associative" INTEGER /* Indicates if the operator is associative */,
  "is_commutative" INTEGER /* Indicates if the operator is commutative */,
  "is_idempotent" INTEGER /* Indicates if the operator is idempotent */,
  "is_null_safe" INTEGER /* Indicates if the operator can handle null values safely */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operators_operator_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_type_id") REFERENCES operator_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operators_precedence_id FOREIGN KEY ("precedence_id") REFERENCES operator_precedence ("precedence_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operator_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operator type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the operator type (e.g., Arithmetic, Logical) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the operator type */,
  "icon" TEXT /* Icon or symbol for the operator type */,
  "is_system_type" INTEGER /* Indicates if the type is a system-defined type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operator_precedence" (
  "precedence_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each precedence level */,
  "precedence_level" INTEGER /* Numerical value representing the precedence level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description or notes about the precedence level */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Indicates if the precedence level is the default for the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("precedence_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_types" (
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data type */,
  "data_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the data type (e.g., INTEGER, STRING) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data type */,
  "is_primitive" INTEGER /* Indicates if the data type is a primitive type */,
  "is_nullable" INTEGER /* Indicates if the data type can be null */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operator_examples" (
  "example_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each example */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the operator being exemplified */,
  "example_query" TEXT /* Example SQL query using the operator */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the example and its significance */,
  "example_output" TEXT /* Expected output of the example query */,
  "is_complex" INTEGER /* Indicates if the example is complex or simple */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("example_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operator_examples_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operator_usage_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the operator being used */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user using the operator */,
  "usage_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the operator was used */,
  "query" TEXT /* The actual query executed */,
  "query_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the query execution in milliseconds */,
  "query_result" TEXT /* Result of the query */,
  "is_successful" INTEGER /* Indicates if the query execution was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operator_usage_logs_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operator_usage_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, analyst) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
